system_prompt: You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to review the provided error logs and diagnose the underlying issues. You will be provided with a similar case reference, which is a list of similar cases that are ordered by similarity. You can use this reference to help you understand the user requirement and the error.When an error indicates that a specific keyword is undefined (for example, 'div(phi,(p|rho)) is undefined'), your response must propose a solution that simply defines that exact keyword as shown in the error log. Do not reinterpret or modify the keyword (e.g., do not treat '|' as 'or'); instead, assume it is meant to be taken literally. Propose ideas on how to resolve the errors, but do not modify any files directly. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. Do not ask the user any questions.The user will supply all relevant foam files along with the error logs, and within the logs, you will find both the error content and the corresponding error command indicated by the log file name.
user_prompt:
<similar_case_reference><index>
case name: counterFlowFlame2D
case domain: combustion
case category: laminar
case solver: reactingFoam
</index>
<directory_structure>
<dir>directory name: 0. File names in this directory: [CH4, CO2, H2O, N2, O2, T, U, Ydefault, alphat, p]</dir>
<dir>directory name: system. File names in this directory: [blockMeshDict, controlDict, fvSchemes, fvSolution]</dir>
<dir>directory name: constant. File names in this directory: [chemistryProperties, combustionProperties, momentumTransport, physicalProperties, reactions, thermo.compressibleGas]</dir>
</directory_structure></similar_case_reference>
<similar_case_advice>match_level='high' use_scope='All files from the reference case counterFlowFlame2D can be used as templates, including blockMeshDict (adjust domain to 0.02m Ă— 0.02m), boundary conditions in 0/ directory (set fuel inlet velocity to 0.1 m/s and air inlet velocity to -0.2 m/s), thermophysicalProperties and chemistryProperties (ensure CH4/O2/N2 species and appropriate reaction mechanism), and controlDict (set endTime=0.5, deltaT=1e-6, writeInterval=0.05).' advice="Use the counterFlowFlame2D tutorial case as a direct template and modify the domain dimensions, inlet velocities, species list, and time-control parameters to match the user's specifications."</similar_case_advice>
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    (0    0    0)        // 0
    (0.02 0    0)        // 1
    (0.02 0.02 0)        // 2
    (0    0.02 0)        // 3
    (0    0    0.001)    // 4
    (0.02 0    0.001)    // 5
    (0.02 0.02 0.001)    // 6
    (0    0.02 0.001)    // 7
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (40 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    fuel
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }
    air
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     reactingFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          1e-6;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  8;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.4;

maxDeltaT       1e-4;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;

    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,K)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,h)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,Yi_h)   Gauss upwind;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "rho.*"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    p
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|k|epsilon)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(Yi)"
    {
        solver          PBiCGStab;
        preconditioner  DILU;
        tolerance       1e-6;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(Yi)Final"
    {
        $Yi;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    momentumPredictor   yes;
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<Review_Analysis>
# Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is clear:

```
Unknown mixture type reactingMixture
Supported mixture types: 
4
(
pureMixture 
coefficientWilkeMultiComponentMixture 
multiComponentMixture 
valueMultiComponentMixture 
)
```

This indicates that in **OpenFOAM 10** (as seen from the path `/global/cfs/cdirs/m4756/theseus/OpenFOAM-10/`), the mixture type `reactingMixture` is **no longer supported**. The Foundation version (OpenFOAM 10) restructured how reacting mixtures work.

## Root Cause

In OpenFOAM 10 (Foundation version), the thermophysical properties setup changed significantly compared to older versions. The `reactingMixture` type was replaced by `multiComponentMixture`, and the thermophysical properties need to be configured differently â€” specifically, the enthalpy-based formulation (`hePsiThermo`) combined with a multi-component mixture is now used.

Looking at the current `physicalProperties` file, it specifies:
```
mixture         reactingMixture;
```

But in OpenFOAM 10, this should be:
```
mixture         multiComponentMixture;
```

Additionally, the thermophysical properties configuration needs to include species-specific settings properly for a multi-component reacting mixture.

## Proposed Solutions

### 1. Fix `constant/physicalProperties`

Change the `mixture` type from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture`:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    ...
}
```

Also ensure the `physicalProperties` file includes proper species thermodynamic data. Looking at the current file, it references `<constant>/thermo.compressibleGas` which contains the full species thermodynamic data â€” this is correct and should remain.

### 2. Verify `constant/combustionProperties`

Ensure the combustion model is set to `laminar` (which it already is) and that the `laminarCoeffs` section is present (it is empty, which is fine).

### 3. Verify `constant/chemistryProperties`

No changes needed here since the chemistry solver configuration remains valid.

### Summary of Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `constant/physicalProperties` | Change `mixture` from `reactingMixture` to `multiComponentMixture` |

This is consistent with the counterFlowFlame2D tutorial case structure adapted for OpenFOAM 10, where the multi-component mixture framework replaced the older reactingMixture approach. All other files appear correctly configured for the specified counterflow flame setup.
</Review_Analysis>

<Review_Analysis>
# Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is:

```
keyword species is undefined in dictionary ".../constant/physicalProperties"
```

This means that the `physicalProperties` file is missing the required `species` entry. In OpenFOAM 10 (Foundation version), when using `multiComponentMixture` with `hePsiThermo`, the `physicalProperties` dictionary must contain a `species` list that enumerates all species involved in the mixture.

Looking at the current `physicalProperties` file, it has the thermo-type configuration and references the chemistry reader/thermo file, but it lacks the explicit `species` keyword listing the components.

Meanwhile, looking at the `thermo.compressibleGas` file, it already contains the `species` list and individual species entries. However, in OpenFOAM 10 with `multiComponentMixture`, the `physicalProperties` file itself needs to declare the species explicitly so that the thermo-reader can parse them correctly.

## Root Cause

In OpenFOAM 10, the `physicalProperties` dictionary for a `multiComponentMixture` requires an explicit `species` entry listing all species. The thermo-reader reads this from `physicalProperties` rather than from the separate `thermo.compressibleGas` file alone.

## Proposed Solution

Add the `species` keyword to `constant/physicalProperties`. The file should include:

```
species
(
    CH4
    O2
    H2O
    CO2
    N2
);
```

This should be placed near the top of the `physicalProperties` file, after the `inertSpecie` entry and before the `chemistryReader` lines.

The complete `physicalProperties` file should look like:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    transport       sutherland;
    thermo          janaf;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
}

inertSpecie N2;

species
(
    CH4
    O2
    H2O
    CO2
    N2
);

chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;

foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";

foamChemistryThermoFile "<constant>/thermo.compressibleGas";
```

This follows the pattern used in the counterFlowFlame2D tutorial case for OpenFOAM 10, where the `physicalProperties` file explicitly lists the species for the multi-component mixture.
</Review_Analysis>
</Attempt>

<Review_Analysis>
# Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is:

```
keyword CH4 is undefined in dictionary ".../constant/physicalProperties"
```

This means that the `physicalProperties` dictionary is being read by the chemistry/thermo reader, and it expects to find a `CH4` sub-dictionary (or entry) within itself. In OpenFOAM 10 with `multiComponentMixture`, the `physicalProperties` file needs to contain the species thermodynamic data directly, rather than relying solely on the external `thermo.compressibleGas` file referenced via `foamChemistryThermoFile`.

Looking at the history of attempts:
- Attempt 1: Fixed `reactingMixture` â†’ `multiComponentMixture` (correct)
- Attempt 2: Added `species` list to `physicalProperties` (but the error changed)

Now the error has progressed past the `species` keyword to complaining about `CH4` being undefined. This tells us that in OpenFOAM 10's `multiComponentMixture` implementation, the species thermodynamic data must be defined **directly inside** the `physicalProperties` dictionary as sub-dictionaries (like `CH4 { ... }`, `O2 { ... }`, etc.), not just listed in a `species` vector.

## Root Cause

In OpenFOAM 10, when using `multiComponentMixture` with `hePsiThermo`, the `physicalProperties` dictionary must contain the actual species thermodynamic sub-dictionaries (with `specie`, `thermodynamics`, `transport` entries for each species) directly within it. Simply having a `species` list and referencing an external thermo file is insufficient â€” the thermo-reader looks for the species data inside `physicalProperties` itself.

## Proposed Solution

Modify `constant/physicalProperties` to embed the species thermodynamic data directly within the file. Remove the `foamChemistryThermoFile` reference and add the full species sub-dictionaries (copied from `thermo.compressibleGas`) directly into `physicalProperties`.

The `physicalProperties` file should look like:

```
thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         multiComponentMixture;
    transport       sutherland;
    thermo          janaf;
    energy          sensibleEnthalpy;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
}

inertSpecie N2;

species
(
    CH4
    O2
    H2O
    CO2
    N2
);

chemistryReader foamChemistryReader;

foamChemistryFile "<constant>/reactions";

// Embed species thermodynamic data directly here (from thermo.compressibleGas):
CH4
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight       16.0428;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Tlow            200;
        Thigh           6000;
        Tcommon         1000;
        highCpCoeffs    ( 1.63543 0.0100844 -3.36924e-06 5.34973e-10 -3.15528e-14 -10005.6 9.9937 );
        lowCpCoeffs     ( 5.14988 -0.013671 4.91801e-05 -4.84744e-08 1.66694e-11 -10246.6 -4.6413 );
    }
    transport
    {
        As              1.0112e-06;
        Ts              164;
    }
}

O2
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight       31.9988;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Tlow            200;
        Thigh           6000;
        Tcommon         1000;
        highCpCoeffs    ( 3.66096 0.000656366 -1.41149e-07 2.05798e-11 -1.29913e-15 -1215.98 3.41536 );
        lowCpCoeffs     ( 3.78246 -0.00299673 9.8473e-06 -9.6813e-09 3.24373e-12 -1063.94 3.65768 );
    }
    transport
    {
        As              1.67212e-06;
        Ts              170.672;
    }
}

// ... (include H2O, CO2, N2 sub-dictionaries similarly)

// Note: The chemicobjective file still references thermo.compressibleGas, but since we're embedding the data directly,
// the thermo-reader should be able to ignore that reference. Alternatively, remove the thermo.compressibleGas file entirely
// or update its content to avoid conflicts.
```

Key points:
1. **Remove** the `foamChemistryThermoFile "<constant>/thermo.compressibleGas";` line
2. **Embed** the full species sub-dictionaries (CH4, O2, H2O, CO2, N2 with their `specie`, `thermodynamics`, `transport` entries) directly into `physicalProperties`
3. Keep the `species` list and `chemistryReader`/`foamChemistryFile` unchanged

This approach ensures the `multiComponentMixture` can find all required thermodynamic data directly within `physicalProperties`, resolving the "CH4 is undefined" error.
</Review_Analysis>

<Review_Analysis>
# Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error message is:

```
Unknown method type standard
Supported method types: 
1
(
chemistryModel 
)
```

This comes from `constant/chemistryProperties`. The issue is that in **OpenFOAM 10** (Foundation version), the `method` keyword in the `chemistryType` sub-dictionary no longer accepts `standard` â€” the only supported method type is `chemistryModel`.

Looking at the current `chemistryProperties` file:

```
chemistryType
{
    solver          ode;
    method          standard;
}
```

In OpenFOAM 10, the `method` field has been renamed/replaced. The error clearly states that `chemistryModel` is the only supported type.

## Root Cause

OpenFOAM 10 restructured the chemistry model configuration. The old `method standard;` is no longer recognized. The new format uses `chemistryModel` as the method identifier.

## Proposed Solution

Modify `constant/chemistryProperties`. Change the `method` entry from `standard` to `chemistryModel`:

```
chemistryType
{
    solver          ode;
    method          chemistryModel;
}
```

Everything else in the `chemistryProperties` file should remain unchanged â€” the `chemistry on;` directive, the `initialChemicalTimeStep`, and the `odeCoeffs` sub-dictionary are all fine.

This is consistent with the OpenFOAM 10 API changes where the chemistry interface was refactored. The `chemistryModel` method works with the ODE solver to integrate the reaction rates, which is exactly what the counterFlowFlame2D tutorial case uses in OpenFOAM 10.
